rulings.Case Title: PATRICK MALKASSIAN et al VS. HOWARD MULLIN et al

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 15, 2011, line 1. PLAINTIFF PATRICK MALKASSIAN, ELIZABETH NALBANDIAN'S Motion For Leave To Amend Complaint. Plaintiff's motion for leave to file a 3rd amended complaint is granted. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: PATRICK MALKASSIAN et al VS. HOWARD MULLIN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc And Motion To Strike Portions Of The Amended Answer To Second Amended Complaint Of Howard Mullin And Susan Mullin , Individually And As Trustees Of The Mullin Family Trust Dated Oct. 01,1982
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 15, 2011, line 1. PLAINTIFF PATRICK MALKASSIAN, ELIZABETH NALBANDIAN'S Motion To Strike Portions Of The Amended Answer To Second Amended Complaint Of Howard Mullin And Susan Mullin, Individually And As Trustees Of The Mullin Family Trust Dated Oct. 01, 1982. Plaintiff's motion to strike poritions of the amended answer is denied. Plaintiff complains about factual issues which are not proper for determination on a motion to strike. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: FIRST RESOLUTION INVESTMENT CORP. VS. CHAO LU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Claim Of Exemption
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 15, 2011, line 1. Hearing On Claim Of Exemption (of Claimant Chao Lu). Defendant's claim of exemption is granted in part and denied in part. The Sheriff is ordered to release $510.00 to the Judgment Creditor but not to garnish further wages. Defendant is directed to pay Plaintiff $50.00 per month until judgment is paid off. = (302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: PANDA RESTAURANT GROUP, INC. et al VS. PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN, LLP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Production Of Documents On Pltfs' Privelige Log
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 15, 2011, line 3. DEFENDANT PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN, LLP AND PAULA WEBER'S Notice Of Motion To Compel Production Of Documents On Plaintiffs' Privilege Log. Defendant's motion to compel is granted in part. Any communications from Mr. Wilkenson to Panda or its officer and employees that pass along advice provided by Defendant must be turned over to Defendant. Requests for sanction are denied. = (302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: SARAH DUPONT VS. JOHN WAI, TRUSTEE OF THE JOHN E. WAI 1981 LIVING et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 15, 2011, line 2. DEFENDANT JOHN WAI, TRUSTEE OF THE JOHN E. WAI 1981 LIVING, JOHN WAI, AN INDIVIDUAL DOING BUSINESS AS UNITED MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendants' motion to strike is denied. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: SARAH DUPONT VS. JOHN WAI, TRUSTEE OF THE JOHN E. WAI 1981 LIVING et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 15, 2011, line 2. DEFENDANT JOHN WAI, TRUSTEE OF THE JOHN E. WAI 1981 LIVING, JOHN WAI, AN INDIVIDUAL DOING BUSINESS AS UNITED, BILL HARKINS DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendants demurrer to the 2nd cause of action is sustained with leave to amend. This claim makes legal conclusions. Plaintiff must plead facts to support this cause of action. Defendants Demurrer to the 9th, 11th and 12th causes of action is overruled. These are sufficiently pled. Defendants' demurrer to the 10 cause of action is overruled. Defendant argues questions of fact that are not proper for determination on demurrer. Plaintiff must file her first amended complaint within 20 days. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: JUANA PASCUBELLO VS. URBAN STRATEGIES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion For Complete Consolidation Of Case # Cgc 11 509678 With Cgc-11-509681
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 15, 2011, line 11. PLAINTIFF JUANA PASCUBELLO'S Motion For Complete Consolidation Of Case # CGC 11 509678 With CGC-11-509681. Plaintiff's motion to consolidate is denied. The causes of action and parties between the two cases are different even though they involve the same witnesses. However, the prejudice to defendants by having these cases consolidated far outweighs any judicial economy from consolidation. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: KEITH ENGLISH, AS TRUSTEE OF THE 2009 IRREVOCABLE VS. TOBI.COM, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 15, 2011, line 7. Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction. Plaintiff's order to show cause re: preliminary injunction is denied. Plaintiff has failed to show a likelihood of success on the merits or irreparable harm. However, the court orders defendant to arrange an on-site inspection by plaintiff and his counsel or documents specifically identified in the Management Agreement, the Operations Agreement and California Corp. Code §17058 and 17106 as subject to inspection. The inspection shall be conducted in a single day and at the parties earliest mutual convenience. =(302/LMG)
